config.name: client
config.description: Configuration element to act as a client that connects to an MCP server

parameter.name: clientName
parameter.description: The name that this client will use to identify itself during the protocol initialization phase. Not to be confused with the name given to this config element (which will later be referenced through config-ref parameters). This name doesn't need to be unique

parameter.name: clientVersion
parameter.description: The version that this client will use to identify itself during the protocol initialization phase. This doesn't need to match the connector or mule application version.

parameter.name: name
parameter.description: The identifier of this element used to reference it in other components

parameter.name: expirationPolicy
parameter.description: Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before the runtime considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the platform will expire the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. The runtime will actually purge the instances when it sees it fit.

config.name: server
config.description: Configuration element to act as an MCP server

parameter.name: serverName
parameter.description: The name that this server will use to identify itself during the protocol initialization phase. Not to be confused with the name given to this config element (which will later be referenced through config-ref parameters). This name doesn't need to be unique

parameter.name: serverVersion
parameter.description: The version that this client will use to identify itself during the protocol initialization phase. This doesn't need to match the connector or mule application version.

parameter.name: name
parameter.description: The identifier of this element used to reference it in other components

parameter.name: reconnection
parameter.description: When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment will fail if the test doesn't pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy

parameter.name: reconnection
parameter.description: When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment will fail if the test doesn't pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy

connection.name: sse-server
connection.description: Exposes the MCP server using the SSE transport, as defined in https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2024-11-05/basic/transports/#http-with-sse[] IMPORTANT: This connection type only works if Mule is started with the mule.http.service.implementation=NETTY system property.

parameter.name: requestTimeout
parameter.description: Request timeout

parameter.name: requestTimeoutUnit
parameter.description: Request Timeout unit

parameter.name: listenerConfig
parameter.description: Reference to the http:listener-config used to expose the SSE and messaging endpoints

parameter.name: sseEndpointPath
parameter.description: The endpoint path clients will use to initiate the SSE connection

parameter.name: messagesPath
parameter.description: The path for the messaging endpoint request will be sent to

parameter.name: reconnection
parameter.description: When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment will fail if the test doesn't pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy

connection.name: streamable-http-server
connection.description: Exposes the MCP server using the Streamable Http transport, as defined in https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/transports#streamable-http[]

parameter.name: requestTimeout
parameter.description: Request timeout

parameter.name: requestTimeoutUnit
parameter.description: Request Timeout unit

parameter.name: listenerConfig
parameter.description: Reference to the http:listener-config used to expose the SSE and messaging endpoints

parameter.name: mcpEndpointPath
parameter.description: The endpoint path clients will use to initiate the SSE connection

parameter.name: responseContentType
parameter.description: Whether responses should be sent by initiating a SSE stream or returning a single application/json body.

parameter.name: defaultResponseHeaders
parameter.description: Custom headers to be included on all valid responses (not including rejected invalid requests)

parameter.name: sessionIdleTimeout
parameter.description: Timeout for idle sessions

parameter.name: sessionIdleTimeoutUnit
parameter.description: Time unit qualifying the #sessionIdleTimeout

parameter.name: sessionsObjectStore
parameter.description: An ObjectStore used to store session information. When deploying to multiple replicas, it is recommended to define a specific object store that will be distributed across all of them, either through Hazelcast or OSv2. If not specified, a default transient instance will be created.

parameter.name: reconnection
parameter.description: When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment will fail if the test doesn't pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy

extension.name: MCP
extension.description: Allows Mule applications to act as both MCP servers and clients This BETA version includes support for tools and resources. More protocol features will be added shortly!

operation.name: callTool
operation.description: Invokes a tool in a remote MCP server

parameter.name: toolName
parameter.description: the name of the tool to be invoked

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: the tool's input arguments

parameter.name: config-ref
parameter.description: The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component

parameter.name: target
parameter.description: The name of a variable on which the operation's output will be placed

parameter.name: targetValue
parameter.description: An expression that will be evaluated against the operation's output and the outcome of that expression will be stored in the target variable

parameter.name: errorMappings
parameter.description: Set of error mappings

parameter.name: reconnectionStrategy
parameter.description: A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors

operation.name: listResources
operation.description: Lists all the available resources in the remote MCP Server

parameter.name: config-ref
parameter.description: The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component

parameter.name: streamingStrategy
parameter.description: Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behaviour

parameter.name: target
parameter.description: The name of a variable on which the operation's output will be placed

parameter.name: targetValue
parameter.description: An expression that will be evaluated against the operation's output and the outcome of that expression will be stored in the target variable

parameter.name: errorMappings
parameter.description: Set of error mappings

parameter.name: reconnectionStrategy
parameter.description: A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors

operation.name: listTools
operation.description: Lists all the available tools in the remote MCP server

parameter.name: config-ref
parameter.description: The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component

parameter.name: streamingStrategy
parameter.description: Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behaviour

parameter.name: target
parameter.description: The name of a variable on which the operation's output will be placed

parameter.name: targetValue
parameter.description: An expression that will be evaluated against the operation's output and the outcome of that expression will be stored in the target variable

parameter.name: errorMappings
parameter.description: Set of error mappings

parameter.name: reconnectionStrategy
parameter.description: A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors

parameter.name: config-ref
parameter.description: The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component

parameter.name: target
parameter.description: The name of a variable on which the operation's output will be placed

parameter.name: targetValue
parameter.description: An expression that will be evaluated against the operation's output and the outcome of that expression will be stored in the target variable

parameter.name: errorMappings
parameter.description: Set of error mappings

parameter.name: reconnectionStrategy
parameter.description: A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors

operation.name: readResource
operation.description: Reads a resource referenced by its URI

parameter.name: uri
parameter.description: A valid URI

parameter.name: config-ref
parameter.description: The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component

parameter.name: target
parameter.description: The name of a variable on which the operation's output will be placed

parameter.name: targetValue
parameter.description: An expression that will be evaluated against the operation's output and the outcome of that expression will be stored in the target variable

parameter.name: errorMappings
parameter.description: Set of error mappings

parameter.name: reconnectionStrategy
parameter.description: A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors

parameter.name: primaryNodeOnly
parameter.description: Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when runnning in Cluster

parameter.name: config-ref
parameter.description: The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component

parameter.name: redeliveryPolicy
parameter.description: Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message

parameter.name: reconnectionStrategy
parameter.description: A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors

source.name: ResourceListener
source.description: Exposes a resource which content is generated by the execution of the owning flow. The flow will be executed for every received resources/read request. The user can choose to implement the flow so that the resource content is static or to make it dynamic. For instance, a dynamic resource could be a expenses.csv file, that is actually the result of executing a live DB query. Or a static resource could be a file obtained through Amazon S3 and locally cached using the <ee:cache> component.

parameter.name: name
parameter.description: The resource name

parameter.name: description
parameter.description: A detailed enough description for an LLM to determine when this resource should be read

parameter.name: uri
parameter.description: The resource URI

parameter.name: resourceMimeType
parameter.description: The resource mime type

parameter.name: primaryNodeOnly
parameter.description: Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when runnning in Cluster

parameter.name: config-ref
parameter.description: The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component

parameter.name: redeliveryPolicy
parameter.description: Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message

parameter.name: reconnectionStrategy
parameter.description: A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors

parameter.name: resourceListenerContent
parameter.description: Defines the resource content and type. These parameters will be evaluated when the flow finishes successfully (payload and variables available)

source.name: ToolListener
source.description: Exposes a tool which result is generated by the execution of the owning flow. The flow will be executed for every received tools/call request. When triggered, the flow will be executed with a message which payload is a Java map holding the input arguments. The tool response is generated when the flow finishes either successfully or with error. In both cases, the response can include several content items of different types. If no error response is defined, then a text content item holding the error's description will be generated automatically

parameter.name: name
parameter.description: The tool's name

parameter.name: description
parameter.description: A detailed enough description for an LLM to determine when this tool should be called

parameter.name: parametersSchema
parameter.description: A JSON schema defining an object which fields will act as the tool's input arguments

parameter.name: primaryNodeOnly
parameter.description: Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when runnning in Cluster

parameter.name: config-ref
parameter.description: The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component

parameter.name: redeliveryPolicy
parameter.description: Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message

parameter.name: reconnectionStrategy
parameter.description: A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors

parameter.name: responses
parameter.description: The response items when the flow finishes successfully

parameter.name: onErrorResponses
parameter.description: the content items when the flow finishes in error

type.name: com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.client.resource.ResourceMetadata
type.description:
Describes a resource properties, without specifying its content.
@since 0.1.0

parameter.name: uri
parameter.description: The resource URI

parameter.name: name
parameter.description: The resource name

parameter.name: description
parameter.description: The resource description

parameter.name: mimeType
parameter.description: The resource mimeType

type.name: com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.client.tool.CallToolResponse
type.description:
The response obtained from calling a tool as a client
@since 0.1.0

parameter.name: contents
parameter.description: The contents generated by the tool

parameter.name: error
parameter.description: Whether the tool execution was successful or errored out. Notice that even in case of error, contents could have been generated

type.name: com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.client.tool.ResourceContent

parameter.name: uri
parameter.description: The resource URI

parameter.name: mimeType
parameter.description: The resource mimeType

parameter.name: text
parameter.description: The content of a text resource. Null for blob ones

parameter.name: blob
parameter.description: The content of a blob resource. Null for text ones.

type.name: com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.client.tool.ToolMetadata
type.description:
Describes a tool through its properties
@since 0.1.0

parameter.name: name
parameter.description: The tool name

parameter.name: description
parameter.description: A detailed enough description for an LLM to determine when this tool should be called

parameter.name: inputSchema
parameter.description: A JSON Schema describing the tool's input parameters.

type.name: com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.connection.NewSessionRequest
type.description:
Contains the data associated to a client requiring the creation of a new server session
@since 0.1.0

type.name: com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.server.resource.BlobResource
type.description:
Defines the contents of a Blob Resource
@since 0.1.0

parameter.name: blob
parameter.description: The raw blob content, without base64 encoding.

type.name: com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.server.resource.Resource
type.description:
Base contract for defining the contents of an MCP Resource
@since 0.1.0

type.name: com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.server.resource.ResourceRequest
type.description:
An inbound request for a resource, used when the connector is acting as an MCP server
@since 0.1.0

parameter.name: requestedUri
parameter.description: The URI for the requested resource

parameter.name: text
parameter.description: The text content

type.name: com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.server.tool.BlobResourceToolResponseContent
type.description:
Defines an embedded blob resource that's part of the response of a Tool implemented through this connector
@since 0.1.0

parameter.name: audience
parameter.description: Describes who the intended customer of this data is. It can include multiple entries to indicate content useful for multiple audiences.

parameter.name: priority
parameter.description: Describes how important this data is for operating the server. A value of 1 means "most important" and indicates that the data is effectively required, while 0 means "least important" and indicates that the data is entirely optional

parameter.name: blob
parameter.description: The raw resource content, without base64 encoding

parameter.name: uri
parameter.description: The resource URI

parameter.name: mimeType
parameter.description: The resource mimeType

type.name: com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.server.tool.ImageToolResponseContent
type.description: Defines an image content that's part of the response of a tool implemented through this connector

parameter.name: audience
parameter.description: Describes who the intended customer of this data is. It can include multiple entries to indicate content useful for multiple audiences.

parameter.name: priority
parameter.description: Describes how important this data is for operating the server. A value of 1 means "most important" and indicates that the data is effectively required, while 0 means "least important" and indicates that the data is entirely optional

parameter.name: data
parameter.description: The raw image content, without base64 encoding

parameter.name: mimeType
parameter.description: The image mimeType

type.name: com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.server.tool.TextResourceToolResponseContent
type.description:
Defines an embedded text resource that's part of the response of a Tool implemented through this connector
@since 0.1.0

parameter.name: audience
parameter.description: Describes who the intended customer of this data is. It can include multiple entries to indicate content useful for multiple audiences.

parameter.name: priority
parameter.description: Describes how important this data is for operating the server. A value of 1 means "most important" and indicates that the data is effectively required, while 0 means "least important" and indicates that the data is entirely optional

parameter.name: uri
parameter.description: The resource URI

parameter.name: mimeType
parameter.description: The resource mimeType

parameter.name: text
parameter.description: The resource text

type.name: com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.server.tool.TextToolResponseContent
type.description:
Defines a text content that's part of the response of a tool implemented through this connector
@since 0.1.0

parameter.name: audience
parameter.description: Describes who the intended customer of this data is. It can include multiple entries to indicate content useful for multiple audiences.

parameter.name: priority
parameter.description: Describes how important this data is for operating the server. A value of 1 means "most important" and indicates that the data is effectively required, while 0 means "least important" and indicates that the data is entirely optional

parameter.name: text
parameter.description: The content text

type.name: com.mulesoft.connectors.mcp.api.server.tool.ToolResponseContent
type.description:
Base class for a Tool response content implemented through this connector
@since 0.1.0

parameter.name: audience
parameter.description: Describes who the intended customer of this data is. It can include multiple entries to indicate content useful for multiple audiences.

parameter.name: priority
parameter.description: Describes how important this data is for operating the server. A value of 1 means "most important" and indicates that the data is effectively required, while 0 means "least important" and indicates that the data is entirely optional
